persona.name: Elijah Foster

occupation.title: Certified Personal Trainer
occupation.description: Trains 4-6 clients per day, designs workout programs, and leads occasional group fitness classes. His work is physical and social, built around helping people feel better about themselves. Recently started viewing skincare as part of the same holistic wellness approach he preaches to clients.

persona.education: Some college — completed one year at Colorado State before dropping out to pursue personal training certification (NASM). No regrets about leaving college. Learns best by doing, not sitting in lectures.
long_term_goals: Open his own boutique fitness and wellness studio
Build a personal brand around holistic men's wellness
Develop a simple, evidence-based skincare + supplement regimen he can recommend to clients
Compete in a natural bodybuilding show within the next 2 years
persona.style: Elijah is upbeat, motivational, and physically present — the kind of person who makes eye contact and gives firm handshakes. He talks with the confident energy of someone used to leading people through hard things. He dresses in fitted athletic wear — Nike, Lululemon — and keeps himself groomed. His interest in skincare is newer and genuine, driven by the same self-improvement mindset that drives his fitness. He asks a lot of questions and absorbs information like a sponge. His communication style is encouraging and positive, sometimes to a fault.

traits: Enthusiastic about self-improvement in all forms
Open-minded and curious — always learning something new
Naturally motivational — can't help but encourage people
Somewhat suggestible to wellness trends before vetting them
Disciplined about fitness but still developing discipline in skincare
Socially confident and comfortable in his own skin

interests: Men's skincare and grooming as part of wellness
Photography — mostly nature and fitness content
likes: Products that feel 'clean' and align with his health-first mindset
Simple routines he can explain to other guys
Brands that market to men without being cringey about it
Learning the science behind why products work
When skincare makes him look more refreshed for client sessions
Morning routines that set a positive tone for the day
Products recommended by dermatologists on podcasts
dislikes: Products that feel greasy or heavy on his skin
Overly gendered marketing that feels patronizing
Skincare routines with more than 4-5 steps
Ingredients he can't pronounce or research easily
Brands that don't align with a health-conscious lifestyle
Being mocked for caring about his skin as a guy
Products that cause breakouts after gym sessions

beliefs: Taking care of your skin is no different from taking care of your body
Wellness is holistic — fitness, nutrition, skincare, mental health are all connected
Men should feel comfortable investing in their appearance
Start simple, build up, and be consistent — same as fitness
skills: Breaking complex health topics into simple, actionable steps
Motivating others to build consistent habits
Basic content creation for social media

general: Started his skincare journey 6 months ago after a Huberman Lab episode on skin health
Currently using a basic routine: cleanser, vitamin C, moisturizer, SPF
Asks his female clients what products they use — genuinely curious
Watches YouTube dermatologist videos while meal-prepping

morning: Cleanser, vitamin C serum, moisturizer, mineral SPF
workday: Trains clients back-to-back from 7 AM to 1 PM
Quick face wash and moisturizer refresh between blocks
evening: Cleanser after his last shower of the day
Moisturizer — considering adding retinol but hasn't committed yet
Winds down with a podcast or a chapter of whatever book he's reading
weekend: Saturday morning hike — often invites clients or friends
Researches new wellness topics — lately it's been skincare ingredients

health.physical: Excellent physical condition. Trains 6 days a week. Occasional post-workout breakouts on his forehead and back, which is what initially pushed him to look into skincare. Sweats heavily, which he's learning to manage with proper cleansing. Takes creatine, protein powder, and vitamin D.
health.mental: Positive and mentally resilient. Has a growth mindset that extends to all areas. Occasionally worries about the financial instability of personal training. Meditates 10 minutes daily — picked it up from a podcast.

relationships.name: Tamara Foster
relationships.description: Single mom who works as a dental hygienist in Fort Collins. Proud of Elijah's career even though she wished he'd finished college. She has great skin and Elijah recently asked her what she uses.

relationships.name: James Foster
relationships.description: College student at CSU. Looks up to Elijah. Elijah is trying to get him on a skincare routine but James is resistant.

relationships.name: Natalie Kim
relationships.description: Works at the same Equinox. She's Korean-American with an extensive skincare routine and has become Elijah's unofficial skincare mentor. She introduced him to vitamin C serum.

relationships.name: Coach Mike Delgado
relationships.description: Head trainer at Equinox who took Elijah under his wing. In his 40s, also recently got into skincare. They discuss wellness trends during slow hours.
